sql_array: CREATE TABLE sequences (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), state INT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO sequences (id, name, state, created_at) VALUES (1, 'seq1', 10, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (2, 'seq2', NULL, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (3, 'seq3', 5, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT sequence_get_all((SELECT ARRAY_AGG(state) FROM sequences WHERE state IS NOT NULL)) AS states WHERE sameTerm('seq1', (SELECT name FROM sequences WHERE id = 1));
SELECT state FROM sequences WHERE sameTerm(name, 'seq2') HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT name, state FROM sequences WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND state IS NOT NULL ORDER BY state DESC;
CREATE TABLE other_sequences (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE, state_float FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO other_sequences (id, name, state_float, created_at) VALUES (1, 'seqA', 10.5, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (2, 'seqB', 0.0, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (3, 'seqC', NULL, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT * FROM other_sequences WHERE sameTerm(name, 'seqA') OR (state_float < 0.1 AND state_float IS NOT NULL);
SELECT sequence_get_all() AS all_states WHERE sameTerm((SELECT name FROM sequences WHERE id = 3), 'seq3');
SELECT state FROM (SELECT state FROM sequences) AS subquery WHERE sameTerm((SELECT name FROM sequences WHERE id = 1), 'seq1') AND state IS NOT NULL;
SELECT name, COUNT(*) FROM sequences GROUP BY name HAVING COUNT(name) > 1;
SELECT * FROM sequences WHERE created_at >= (SELECT MIN(created_at) FROM sequences GROUP BY state HAVING state IS NOT NULL);
